stem: Triangles MNO and PQR are similar, where vertices M, N, O correspond to P, Q, R, respectively. Angle N is a right angle and the measure of angle M is 40°. Which of the following ratios is equal to sin(40°)?
rationale:
Choice C is correct. In right triangle MNO, the right angle is at N, so MO is the hypotenuse. The side opposite the 40° angle at M is NO. By definition, sin(40°) equals (opposite)/(hypotenuse) = NO/MO.

Choice A gives adjacent/hypotenuse = cos(40°). Choice B is the reciprocal of cos(40°). Choice D is opposite/adjacent = tan(40°).
